findings.description: Health Check 2GP package missing namespace configuration. The main package has namespace 'elaro' (line 20) but the Health Check package directory (force-app-healthcheck) has no namespace defined. The _TODO_HC_namespace comment (line 22) indicates 'elaroHC' should be registered but is not yet configured. Additionally, the Health Check package has no entry in packageAliases.
findings.recommendation: Register 'elaroHC' namespace in DevHub and add it to the Health Check package directory configuration in sfdx-project.json. Add the Health Check 0Ho package ID to packageAliases.

findings.description: Main package alias uses placeholder ID '0HoXXXXXXXXXXXX'. This must be replaced with the real Package2 ID before creating package versions. Health Check package has no alias at all.
findings.recommendation: Create both packages in DevHub using 'sf package create' and update packageAliases with real 0Ho IDs for both 'elaro' and 'Elaro Health Check'.

findings.description: Custom object Elaro_Async_Framework_Flags__c exists in the objects directory but is NOT included in any permission set. Neither Elaro_Async_Admin nor Elaro_Async_User permission sets grant access to this Hierarchy Custom Setting. Users will be unable to view or modify async framework feature flags after package installation. This is the ONLY custom object (out of 53) missing from permission sets.
findings.recommendation: Add objectPermissions for Elaro_Async_Framework_Flags__c to Elaro_Async_Admin.permissionset-meta.xml (CRUD + viewAll) and Elaro_Async_User.permissionset-meta.xml (read-only). Also add fieldPermissions for any custom fields on this object.

findings.description: 22 of 57 LWC components in the main package contain hardcoded English strings in HTML templates instead of Custom Labels. Top offenders: complianceGraphViewer (14 strings), elaroROICalculator (9), controlMappingMatrix (8), jiraIssueCard (8), remediationSuggestionCard (8), escalationPathConfig (6), elaroDashboard (5), elaroEventExplorer (5), elaroEventMonitor (5), jiraCreateModal (5). Approximately 101 hardcoded strings total across all 22 components. This violates AppExchange i18n requirements.
findings.recommendation: Replace all hardcoded English text with Custom Label imports. Create new labels following the naming convention [MODULE]_[Description] (e.g., GRAPH_Framework, GRAPH_Policy, ROI_Title, etc.). Add labels to force-app/main/default/labels/CustomLabels.labels-meta.xml with <protected>true</protected>. Priority: complianceGraphViewer, elaroROICalculator, controlMappingMatrix, jiraIssueCard, remediationSuggestionCard.

findings.description: 4 Custom Labels in the main managed package have <protected>false</protected>: AW_AutoScanError (line 498), AW_AutoScanPassCount (line 504), AW_AutoScanFailCount (line 510), AW_AutoScanNoFindings (line 518). All other 159 labels are correctly set to <protected>true</protected>. Unprotected labels in managed packages can be modified by subscribers, causing merge conflicts on upgrades and unpredictable behavior.
findings.recommendation: Change <protected>false</protected> to <protected>true</protected> for all 4 labels: AW_AutoScanError, AW_AutoScanPassCount, AW_AutoScanFailCount, AW_AutoScanNoFindings.

findings.description: Hardcoded external endpoint 'https://api.originstamp.com/v4/timestamp' found. External integrations must use Named Credentials for AppExchange security review. While 4 Named Credentials exist (Elaro_Claude_API, Jira_API, Slack_Webhook, Teams_Webhook), OriginStamp is not covered.
findings.recommendation: Create a Named Credential 'OriginStamp_API' (OriginStamp_API.namedCredential-meta.xml) and replace the hardcoded URL with 'callout:OriginStamp_API/v4/timestamp'.

findings.description: Hardcoded external endpoint 'https://events.pagerduty.com/v2/enqueue' found in PagerDutyIntegration.cls. The same hardcoded URL also appears in ElaroComplianceAlert.cls (line 411). No Named Credential exists for PagerDuty.
findings.recommendation: Create a Named Credential 'PagerDuty_Events' (PagerDuty_Events.namedCredential-meta.xml) and replace both hardcoded URLs in PagerDutyIntegration.cls and ElaroComplianceAlert.cls with 'callout:PagerDuty_Events/v2/enqueue'.

findings.description: The LWC utils directory contains utils.js without a matching utils.js-meta.xml file. The directory does have focusManager.js with its own focusManager.js-meta.xml. This is the only missing meta.xml across the entire codebase.
findings.recommendation: If utils.js is a shared module imported by other components, add a utils.js-meta.xml with apiVersion 66.0 and isExposed=false. If it is only used as an ES module import, confirm it deploys correctly without the meta file.

findings.description: OrchestrationService has @AuraEnabled properties on inner classes but is not in any permission set. RuleEngineEventBridge similarly has @AuraEnabled inner class properties without permission set coverage. While these may be wrapper classes whose properties are serialized by controllers that ARE in permission sets, they should still be explicitly granted for completeness.
findings.recommendation: Add OrchestrationService to Elaro_Admin classAccesses. Add RuleEngineEventBridge to Elaro_Rule_Engine_Admin classAccesses.

findings.description: ESLint v9 configuration exists for LWC linting. However, no dedicated Salesforce Code Analyzer (sf scanner) configuration file (.code-analyzer.yml or scanner config) was found. The CI pipeline references scanner steps, but local development would benefit from a config file for consistent settings.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a .code-analyzer.yml or scanner configuration for consistent results between local development and CI. Run 'sf scanner run --target force-app --format table --severity-threshold 1' before final packaging.

findings.description: Main package namespace 'elaro' is properly configured in sfdx-project.json (line 20). This was previously flagged as critical (empty namespace) and has been resolved.
findings.recommendation: None - correctly configured. Previous AX-001 critical finding resolved.

findings.description: All Apex class meta.xml files (299 classes) and all LWC js-meta.xml files use API version 66.0 (Spring '26). All Health Check classes and LWC components are also on v66.0. Zero outdated API versions found across the entire codebase.
findings.recommendation: None - all metadata is on the correct API version.

findings.description: All Apex classes have matching .cls-meta.xml files. All triggers have matching .trigger-meta.xml files. All Health Check classes have matching meta.xml. The only missing meta.xml is utils.js in LWC (see AX-008).
findings.recommendation: None - complete meta.xml pairing for Apex code.

findings.description: Permission set structure follows best practices with Admin + User pairs for modules: AI Governance (Admin/User), SEC (Admin/User), Rule Engine (Admin/User), Async (Admin/User), Health Check (Admin/User). Core package has Elaro Admin, Elaro Admin Extended (platform permissions), Elaro User, Elaro Auditor. TechDebt_Manager provides TechDebt access. 15 total permission sets across both packages. All 20 custom tabs are covered. 52 of 53 custom objects are covered. Elaro Admin Extended properly separates platform permissions (ApiEnabled, RunReports, ExportReport, application visibility, schedulers). Previous findings AX-002/003/004 (many objects, tabs, and classes missing) have been resolved.
findings.recommendation: Fix the one remaining missing object (AX-003: Elaro_Async_Framework_Flags__c).

findings.description: 163 Custom Labels defined in the main package across module categories: Copilot (16), UI (12), Scheduler (10), Command Center (22), Assessment Wizard (27), SEC (43), Trust Center (35), AI Governance (25), Rule Engine (18), Async Framework (7). Health Check has 27 additional labels in its own package. Total: 190 labels. All Health Check labels are protected.
findings.recommendation: Fix the 4 unprotected labels (AX-005) and add labels for the 22 LWC components with hardcoded strings (AX-004).

findings.description: 4 Named Credentials configured: Elaro_Claude_API, Jira_API, Slack_Webhook, Teams_Webhook. This covers most external integrations properly.
findings.recommendation: Add Named Credentials for PagerDuty and OriginStamp (AX-006/AX-007).

findings.description: Feature flags are well-implemented. Elaro_Feature_Flags__c has 6 kill switches: AI_Governance_Enabled, Assessment_Wizard_Enabled, Command_Center_Enabled, Event_Monitoring_Enabled, SEC_Module_Enabled, Trust_Center_Enabled. Health Check has its own Health_Check_Feature_Flags__c. Async Framework has its own Elaro_Async_Framework_Flags__c. Previous finding AX-007 (missing feature flags) has been resolved.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding kill switches for Jira Integration and Copilot modules.

findings.description: Health Check 2GP is well-structured: Admin and User permission sets with appropriate CRUD differentiation, 27 Custom Labels (all protected), 1 tab with visibility grants, 1 custom object with field permissions, 13 Apex classes in Admin permission set, 6 LWC components with no hardcoded strings detected.
findings.recommendation: None - Health Check package is AppExchange-ready pending namespace registration.

appexchange_readiness.progress_since_last_review: Significant improvement from Feb 2026 review. Previous critical blocker (missing namespace) resolved. Previous high findings (many objects/tabs/classes missing from permission sets) largely resolved. Feature flags now implemented.
blockers: AX-004: 22 LWC components with ~101 hardcoded English strings - will fail i18n review
AX-006/AX-007: Hardcoded external endpoints (OriginStamp, PagerDuty) - will fail security review
AX-003: Elaro_Async_Framework_Flags__c missing from permission sets - users cannot configure async framework
warnings: AX-005: 4 unprotected Custom Labels in managed package - may cause upgrade issues
AX-001: Health Check namespace not yet registered - blocks HC package creation
AX-002: Placeholder package alias IDs - must be replaced before packaging
AX-009: 2 classes with @AuraEnabled properties not in permission sets
